evidences.title: Binance Historical Tick Data (1 second) for a specific date range and ...
evidences.content: Optimus Prime's user avatar ## 1 Answer 1 Binance provides some of its historical trading data on the `binance.vision` domain. Specifically, the 1 second klines for BTCUSDT pair can be found here: `binance.vision` Here's a sample bash script that downloads and unpacks the raw data for the whole month: `#!/bin/bash for i in $(seq -f "%02g" 1 31) # zero-padded days 01, 02, 03, ... 31 do # month of july 2023, day number is the `i` variable archiveName="BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-${i}.zip" # download the .zip, extract it (saves .csv), and remove the .zip wget " && unzip "./${archiveName}" && rm "./${archiveName}" done` As mentioned in their GitHub readme, each of the the `.csv` lines contains the following field values: `.csv` [...] `.csv` | Open time | Open | High | Low | Close | Volume | Close time | Quote asset volume | Number of trades | Taker buy base asset volume | Taker buy quote asset volume | Ignore | --- --- --- --- --- --- | So for example, the file `BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-01.csv` containing this first line `BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-01.csv` `1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0` enab...

evidences.title: Binance Historical 2017-2025 (all USDT pairs) - Kaggle
evidences.content: ## About Dataset This dataset contains historical cryptocurrency market data extracted from public Binance API endpoints. The files provided include 1-minute (1m) candlestick (kline) data for all USDT trading pairs, downloaded and updated programmatically from 2017 to 2024. Data is stored in efficient Parquet format for compatibility and performance, covering open, high, low, close, and volume for each time interval. This dataset is ideal for quantitative research, backtesting trading strategies, machine learning projects, and market analysis. Source: (public API, no account required) Updated: Regularly (monthly and daily backfill). Columns: timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume, symbol, and more. Coverage: Global cryptocurrency markets. ## Usability info 5.88 [...] arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2017 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2018 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2019 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2020 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2021 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2022 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2023 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2024 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USD...

evidences.content: #### CLI Options | Option | Alias | Description | --- | `--pair` | `-p` | Trading pair (e.g., BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT) | | `--interval` | `-i` | Kline interval (1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w) | | `--start` | `-s` | Start date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--end` | `-e` | End date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--output` | `-o` | Output directory path (filename is auto-generated) | | `--format` | `-f` | Output format (json or csv, default: json) | | `--help` | `-h` | Display help | | `--version` | `-V` | Display version | #### Hybrid mode You can also provide some options and be prompted for the rest: ``` # You will be prompted for start date, end date, and output path # ``` ### Library [...] ## Installation ### Via Homebrew (macOS/Linux) ``` brew install maxgfr/tap/binance-historical ``` ### Via npm ``` npm install -g binance-historical ``` ### Download binary Download the latest binary for your platform from the releases page. ## Usage ### CLI #### Interactive mode ``` binance-historical download ``` #### Non-interactive mode Pass all options as arguments: ``` binance-historical download \ --pair BTCUSDT \ --interval 4h \ --start 2024-01-01 \ --end 2024-12-3...

evidences.content: all_data.extend(data) start_time = data[-1] + 1 else: break time.sleep(0.5) # Delay to prevent exceeding request limits return all_data # Binance timeframes timeframes = ['1m', '3m', '5m', '15m', '30m', '1h', '2h', '4h', '6h', '8h', '12h', '1d', '3d', '1w', '1M'] # Retrieving and saving data for each timeframe for timeframe in timeframes: print(f"Сбор данных для таймфрейма: {timeframe}") data = get_binance_data('BTCUSDT', '01 Jan 2022', '31 Dec 2022', timeframe) filename = f'btcusdt_{timeframe}_2022.csv' with open(filename, 'w', newline='') as file: writer = csv.writer(file) for row in data: writer.writerow(row) print(f"Data saved in {filename}") print("All data has been successfully collected and saved.") [...] The Python Script The script uses Python's requests library to interact with the Binance API. It fetches data within a specified date range and interval (or timeframe) for a given trading pair, such as BTC/USDT. The script is capable of handling multiple timeframes and neatly organizes the fetched data into CSV files for each timeframe. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC") # fetch 30 minute klines for the last month of 2017 klines = client. get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") # fetch weekly klines since it listed klines = client. get_historical_klines("NEOBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1WEEK,"1 Jan, 2017") ``` ## Get Historical Kline/Candlesticks using a generator Fetch klines using a generator ``` for kline in client. get_historical_klines_generator("BNBBTC", Client. evidences.title: Bitcoin above ___ on November 18? - Polymarket Analytics
evidences.content: Logo # Bitcoin above \_\_\_ on November 18? This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar. Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets. Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source. ## Polymarket Analytics Actionable insights for prediction markets. Twitter Logo ### Polymarket Data ### Resources

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to ge...

evidences.title: EST to UTC - Online Converter
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. evidences.content: ### What are the national holidays in the EST time zone? National holidays: New Year's Day: Celebrated on January 1st, marking the start of the new year. Independence Day: Observed on July 4th, with parades, fireworks, and celebrations throughout the region. Labor Day: Celebrated on the first Monday in September, honoring American workers and their contributions. Thanksgiving Day: Observed on the fourth Thursday in November, a major holiday for family gatherings and feasting. Christmas Day: Celebrated on December 25th, marked by family gatherings, feasts, and festive celebrations. ### What is some local trivia or geography about the EST region? [...] ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone? Major industries: Finance: New York City is a global financial hub, home to the New York Stock Exchange and Wall Street. Healthcare: The healthcare and pharmaceutical industries are significant, with major medical institutions like the Mayo Clinic in Rochester. Technology: The zone includes major tech hubs, such as Boston and Washington, D.C., with a focus on biotechnology, software development, and IT services.
